name: ERPSMART Builder Studio AI/RAG Manifest
purpose: Describe the UI-first Builder Studio, backend Control Plane, safe preview workflow, and AI/RAG boundaries for future Builder Agents.
safe_operations: read preview manifests and output text
run end-to-end smoke with temporary DB records and cleanup
edit definition_json.formLayout metadata through Builder Studio
edit definition_json.automation metadata through Builder Studio
follow the manual browser smoke journey with read/save/validate/preview only
generate publish dry-run artifacts under storage
create publish candidate snapshots under storage
forbidden_operations: publish from Builder Studio MVP
write runtime module files directly
run generated module migrations from Builder Studio MVP
edit Core files
edit Warehouse files
edit SaaS, licensing, updater, package, composer, vendor, node_modules, public/build files
bypass validation before preview
treat preview artifacts or vector indexes as source of truth
leave smoke DB records or unique smoke preview directories behind
treat formLayout metadata as runtime-rendered before the renderer exists
execute automation metadata before the Workflow Engine exists
claim publish exists in the Builder Studio MVP
execute publish
treat publish readiness as actual publish
treat publish readiness diagnostic artifact as a runtime module
copy publish dry-run artifacts into runtime paths
copy publish candidate snapshots into runtime paths
approve publish from dry-run review
request publish approval before approval implementation exists
approve, reject, or revoke publish approval
delete runtime modules
uninstall modules
drop tables or business data
hide existing modules without an explicitly approved future lifecycle implementation
future_operations: publish and rollback after safety contracts are complete
hide existing modules through approved gates
uninstall generated modules after dependency analysis, backups, retention rules, and rollback are implemented
remove capabilities after safety contracts and data dependency checks are implemented
notes_for_ai_builder_agent: Use the backend Builder Control Plane APIs instead of editing runtime module files.
Propose changes to definition_json, then call validate and preview.
Respect warning-only capabilities and do not generate unsafe APIs for them.
Never use stale backup, vendor, build, cache, or generated noise as canonical evidence.
The end-to-end smoke path may create temporary DB records, but it must clean them up.
Preview output must confirm Real runtime writes performed: 0.
Form layout metadata is definition data, but runtime form rendering is future work.
Automation metadata is definition data, but runtime workflow/email/task/approval/webhook execution is future work.
The demo journey is safe for manual smoke as long as it remains limited to draft, save, validate, and preview.
Module lifecycle/removal is safety-critical planning only until a separate implementation task adds dependency checks, backups, retention rules, rollback, permissions, jobs, and verifier coverage.
Definition lifecycle MVP can archive, restore, and delete unpublished Builder control-plane records only.
Publish readiness analyzer is read-only and reports dependency impact with writes_performed 0.
Publish readiness diagnostic artifact is derived and not source of truth.
Publish dry-run artifacts are derived review artifacts and not source of truth.
Dry-run review checklist is informational only and does not approve publish.
Publish candidate snapshots are derived review artifacts and not source of truth.
Human approval gate and publish audit log are planning-only; no approval persistence exists.
Control-plane-only delete does not delete runtime modules, generated files, preview artifact directories, migrations, database tables, media, or business data.
Preview remains a safe executable generation operation; publish, uninstall, rollback, table drops, and runtime module removal remain forbidden execution operations.
notes_for_business_operations_agent_sepa: The Business Operations Agent is separate from the AI Builder Agent.
Business operations should use permissioned runtime business APIs after modules exist.
Business operations must not modify Builder schema or module definitions unless explicitly authorized.
